question.text: What is the role of a clearing corporation in the secondary market?

answer.text: Acting as the central counterparty for all trades, guaranteeing settlement even if one party defaults
Setting the prices at which securities are traded
Issuing new securities to the public
Providing credit ratings for listed companies

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Clearing corporations act as the central counterparty (CCP) for all trades executed on the stock exchange. They guarantee settlement by interposing themselves between buyer and seller. All buyers pay funds to the CC, and all sellers deliver securities to the CC. **The Trap:** Clearing corporations don't set prices (option b) - prices are determined by supply and demand in the market. They ensure settlement, not pricing. **Cross-Exam Context:** Key clearing corporations include NSE Clearing Ltd., Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd. (ICCL), and Metropolitan Clearing Corporation of India Ltd. (MCCIL). Interoperability allows clearing at any CC regardless of the exchange used. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clearing corporation = Settlement guarantee. If your counterparty defaults, the CC still ensures you get your shares or money. This is why exchange-traded transactions are safer than OTC.
